desc: Waigeo is one of the main Raja Ampat islands, popular for coral-rich diving, limestone karst scenery, and wildlife encounters.
hls: Raja Ampat coral gardens with exceptional marine biodiversity
Manta ray, reef shark, and turtle sightings on guided snorkel/dives
Piaynemo-style limestone karst panoramas and lagoon lookouts
Dawn bird-of-paradise watching in Waigeo forest habitats
Remote eco-resorts and homestays over clear shallow reefs
Rich macro life and healthy soft corals for underwater photographers

todo.name: Two-tank day trip from Waigeo
todo.desc: Head to guide-selected sites based on current and visibility for pelagic action and reef walls.

todo.name: Friwen or Yenbuba reef drift
todo.desc: Join a local boat for current-aware snorkeling over shallow coral gardens and fish schools.

todo.name: Piaynemo karst climb
todo.desc: Start early for cooler weather and fewer boats at the iconic staircase panorama.

todo.name: Bird-of-paradise dawn trek
todo.desc: Meet local guides before sunrise for forest hides and best chances of courtship displays.

todo.name: Visit a Papuan village homestay
todo.desc: Learn local fishing rhythms, sago-based meals, and community-led conservation projects.

todo.name: Paddle mangrove channels
todo.desc: Use calm morning windows to kayak through limestone-fringed inlets and hidden coves.

prac.visa: Check latest entry requirements based on your passport before booking.
alerts: Heavy rain and choppy seas (often June to September) can delay speedboats and reduce underwater visibility.
Inter-island logistics depend on Sorong arrivals; missed domestic connections may force overnight stays.
Diving permits, marine park fees, and boat fuel surcharges can significantly raise trip costs.
Cash access is limited outside Sorong/Waisai; many remote properties do not accept cards reliably.

wifi.note: Internet on Waigeo is usable in Sorong-facing resorts and some homestays, but many islands rely on patchy mobile signal, generator hours, and weather-affected links.

prac.sim: Buy an eSIM/local SIM in arrival city for best coverage and data value.
prac.safety: Use standard travel precautions and follow local weather/sea-condition guidance.
prac.lang: Indonesian (Bahasa Indonesia) plus local Papuan languages
prac.fltNote: No direct London departures; practical routing is UK to Jakarta/Manado/Makassar, onward to Sorong (SOQ), then ferry or speedboat to Waigeo.
